author: @musikSkool
text: My problem with Fusion is that it isn't unlimited free energy.  When we start making them it will costs billions to make and maintain a fusion power plant that no one is going to build one in a 3rd world country for a very, very long time.  And your electric bill will never reach zero, it will always cost something to keep the reactor running.

author: @calvingreene90
text:
The fusion power plant that we could build today is a large spherical steel tank filled with water at great pressure. You set off a high fusion yield device heating the water without generating steam (why the very high pressure necessary) you generate electricity from cooling the tank. You recover the fissionables and other debris for building future fusion triggers.
You have to go big for it to be cost effective.

author: @calvingreene90
text: The conditions in a fusion reactor have to be more extreme than in a star. The sun's energy density is that of a good compost pile.

author: @CCoburn3
text: There are people who do not seem to realize that there is no chemical reaction that produces fusion. As you point out in another of your videos (i.e., the one on catalysts), chemical reactions are reactions between atoms at the level of electrons. Fusion, on the other hand, is a reaction at the level of the nucleus.  So, while cold fusion is possible, it is not caused by a chemical reaction. One way to tell if you have achieved fusion is to see if the reaction produces neutrons. No neutron -- no fusion. (At least for fusion of hydrogen atoms.) That's why fakers won't allow people to get near their devices with a neutron detection apparatus.

author: @Ken00001010
text: Another very good presentation. Spacecraft engines are the leading application for fusion because the cost of the equipment is not very important, whereas, the mass of the fuel per unit of output energy is critical. Also, in space you don't care much about radioactive fuel or radioactive waste products. There is not so much worry about scams in space because nobody is going to get on top of a rocket that runs on phony-baloney.
